notes: Phase A.2 spike — captured against the real Codex SDK + CLI (subscription auth via ~/.codex/auth.json).
Two probes: (1) thread.runStreamed() to capture the event-stream shape, (2) thread.run() to confirm the non-streaming Turn shape.
The streamed probe asked the agent a question that REQUIRED tool use to answer (file listing); the run probe asked a question answerable from prompt alone.

Confirms (a) outputSchema delivers schema-validated JSON in turn.finalResponse, (b) Codex.config can carry forced_login_method + show_raw_agent_reasoning + model_reasoning_effort as CLI --config overrides, (c) ThreadOptions.sandboxMode=read-only + approvalPolicy=never lets the agent run shell commands but not write files.

WARNING: even with networkAccessEnabled: false AND sandboxMode: read-only, the agent CAN execute shell commands locally (read-only sandbox blocks WRITES only). The Codex adapter relies on read-only sandbox + no-network defense-in-depth, not on disabling tool use entirely.

Re-spike is manual (not automated): construct a Codex client + thread + run() against this repo with subscription auth and capture event shapes. Phase A did not add a spike script under tools/agent-review/src/spikes/ because the SDK shape is stable enough that ad-hoc re-spike (similar to how this fixture was produced) is sufficient. A future cycle that adds a Makefile target should land the script under tools/agent-review/src/spikes/codex-models-list-2026-05.ts alongside the gemini/grok siblings.

CodexOptions.config: CodexConfigObject — flattened into --config key=value CLI args. e.g., {model_reasoning_effort:'high', forced_login_method:'chatgpt'}
CodexOptions.env: Record<string,string> (replaces process.env for the CLI subprocess; NOT merged)

Thread.id: string | null — populated AFTER first turn.runStreamed/.run resolves

Turn.items: ThreadItem[] — aggregated items emitted during the turn
Turn.finalResponse: string — last agent_message text (schema-validated JSON when outputSchema set)

TurnOptions.outputSchema: unknown — JSON Schema object enforcing structured response shape
